rationale_for_baseline_tier: An uncomplicated external haemorrhoid is low-acuity and self-managed with fibre and topical measures, BUT rectal bleeding cannot be examined via chat and always warrants an in-person perianal exam and age-appropriate bowel-cancer screening, so prompt (routine-to-prompt) review is appropriate (T2). Thrombosis/heavy bleeding routes to ED (T4); colorectal alarm features route to GP/colonoscopy.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Blood mixed through the stool or black tarry stools, a persistent change in bowel habit, thinner stools, weight loss, or family history of bowel cancer
The lump becomes hard, dark purple and excruciatingly painful, or bleeding becomes heavy/dripping into the bowl, or you feel faint or feverish
escalation_edges.rationale: Thrombosed haemorrhoid (may need incision) or significant bleeding.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the lump becomes hard, dark purple and excruciatingly painful, or the bleeding becomes heavy and drips into the bowl, or you feel faint or feverish — go to the Emergency Department promptly.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone; very anxious about the bleeding
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Reassure while being clear that PR bleeding still needs an in-person check and screening.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Increase fibre and fluids, use a footstool and don't strain or linger on the toilet, and use the cream short-term. The bleeding and itch should settle in a few days. Please still arrange an in-person check of the area and bowel-cancer screening if due.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Self-care + in-person GP exam and screening; ED for thrombosis/heavy bleeding
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: ED for heavy bleeding, an excruciating hard lump, faintness or fever.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Uncomplicated external haemorrhoid with outlet bleeding; colorectal alarm features and thrombosis screened negative; needs in-person exam/anoscopy and age-appropriate screening; fibre + topical.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Attributing bleeding to haemorrhoids with no alarm-feature screen, exam or screening arranged.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A colorectal cancer is missed because all bleeding was blamed on haemorrhoids.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED/ambulance for light outlet bleeding with a benign soft lump — usually unnecessary.
